questions.q: The structure which receives the signals from the sensory receptors is the (a) Dorsal root ganglion (b) Ventral root ganglion (c) Dendrite (d) Axon
What is the function of the spinal cord in the case of sudden withdrawal of the hand from a hot surface?
questions.answer: The spinal cord acts as a reflex centre to protect the body from injury.

What is the term for the ability of the nervous system to change its response to a stimulus when it is repeated?
The phenomenon of the decrease in the sensitivity of a sense organ to a constant stimulus is called
What is the function of the cerebellum in the human body?
questions.answer: The cerebellum coordinates the voluntary movements of the body including posture, balance, equilibrium, and muscle tone.

questions.q: The structure which receives the signals from the sensory receptors is the ___________.
In the case of a person hearing a bell ringing loudly for the first time, what is the response of the auditory nerve?
questions.answer: The auditory nerve responds with the maximum intensity.

questions.q: What is the term for the process in which the nervous system responds to a stimulus by bringing the body into a state of readiness for immediate action?
The structure which contains the cell bodies of the sensory neurons is the ___________.
